PROP_001.name: Passcode_Never_Transmitted
PROP_001.verification_notes: SPAKE2+ protocol by design never transmits passcode. Only w0, w1, pA, pB, cA, cB transmitted.

PROP_002.name: Initiator_Random_Generation
PROP_002.verification_notes: T01 action uses Crypto_DRBG(256). Specification requires 32-byte random.

PROP_003.name: Responder_Random_Generation
PROP_003.verification_notes: T05 action uses Crypto_DRBG(256). Specification requires 32-byte random.

PROP_004.name: Session_Identifier_Uniqueness
PROP_004.verification_notes: Guard conditions enforce session ID range and uniqueness.

PROP_005.name: PasscodeID_Verification
PROP_005.specification_quote: On receipt of PBKDFParamRequest, the responder SHALL: 1. Verify passcodeID is set to 0.
PROP_005.verification_notes: T04 guard: msg.passcodeId == 0. Matches spec SHALL.

PROP_006.name: cB_Verification_By_Initiator
PROP_006.specification_quote: On receipt of Pake2, the initiator SHALL: ... Verify cB
PROP_006.verification_notes: T11/T12 guards verify cB matches computed value.

PROP_007.name: cA_Verification_By_Responder
PROP_007.specification_quote: On receipt of Pake3, the responder SHALL: ... Verify cA
PROP_007.verification_notes: R_ProcessingPake3 verifies cA matches computed value.

PROP_008.name: No_Encrypted_Data_Before_PakeFinished
PROP_008.verification_notes: Invariant: session_keys_installed=false in all pre-completion states.

PROP_009.name: Session_Keys_Derived_From_Ke
PROP_009.specification_quote: SessionKeys = Crypto_KDF(inputKey = Ke, salt = [], info = SEKeys_Info, len = 3*CRYPTO_SYMMETRIC_KEY_LENGTH_BITS)

PROP_010.name: Initiator_Uses_I2RKey_For_Encryption
PROP_010.specification_quote: The initiator SHALL use I2RKey to encrypt and integrity protect messages and the R2IKey to decrypt and verify messages.
PROP_010.verification_notes: FSM state I_SessionEstablished includes both keys. Structure implicitly models correct usage.

PROP_011.name: Responder_Uses_R2IKey_For_Encryption
PROP_011.specification_quote: The responder SHALL use R2IKey to encrypt and integrity protect messages and the I2RKey to decrypt and verify messages.
PROP_011.verification_notes: FSM state R_SessionEstablished includes both keys. Structure implicitly models correct usage.

PROP_012.name: AttestationChallenge_Usage_Restriction
PROP_012.specification_quote: The AttestationChallenge SHALL only be used as a challenge during device attestation.
PROP_012.verification_notes: Usage constraint beyond FSM scope. Requires runtime behavior analysis.

PROP_015.name: PASE_Only_During_Commissioning
PROP_015.specification_quote: PASE SHALL only be used for session establishment mechanism during device commissioning.
PROP_015.verification_notes: T02 guard: commissioning_window_open == true. FSM enforces commissioning context.

PROP_016.name: PBKDF_Iterations_In_Valid_Range
PROP_016.verification_notes: GAP_001 CONFIRMED: Responder generates valid params, but initiator does NOT validate received params. Page 169 shows no validation between PBKDFParamResponse and Pake1.

PROP_017.name: PBKDF_Salt_Length_Requirement
PROP_017.specification_quote: salt: A random value per device of at least 16 bytes and at most 32 bytes
PROP_017.verification_notes: Similar to PROP_016: No initiator validation of received salt length.

PROP_018.name: Transcript_Binding
PROP_018.verification_notes: T09 action: Crypto_Transcript() binds all protocol messages.

PROP_020.name: Same_Message_Exchange
PROP_020.verification_notes: All PASE messages in single exchange.

PROP_021.name: Encrypted_Status_Reports
PROP_021.verification_notes: Message encryption layer property. Beyond FSM scope.

PROP_026.name: Message_Counter_Window_Replay_Protection
PROP_026.verification_notes: Replay protection via message counter window.

PROP_027.name: Ke_Secrecy
PROP_027.verification_notes: SPAKE2+ protocol property. Ke never transmitted.

PROP_028.name: I2RKey_Secrecy
PROP_028.verification_notes: Derived from Ke via HKDF. Never transmitted.

PROP_029.name: R2IKey_Secrecy
PROP_029.verification_notes: Derived from Ke via HKDF. Never transmitted.

PROP_031.name: Session_Key_Freshness
PROP_031.verification_notes: Fresh randoms per session ensure key freshness.

PROP_032.name: Protocol_Message_Sequence
PROP_032.verification_notes: FSM enforces: PBKDFParamRequest → Response → Pake1 → Pake2 → Pake3 → Finished

PROP_036.name: Reserved_Tags_Ignored
PROP_036.verification_notes: Reserved tags in TLV ignored per spec.

PROP_040.name: Session_Resource_Eviction
PROP_040.specification_quote: responder SHALL evict an existing session using SessionTimestamp LRU

PROP_041.name: Busy_Response_Retry_Delay_Enforcement
PROP_041.specification_quote: The BUSY StatusReport SHALL set the ProtocolData to a 16-bit value indicating the minimum time in milliseconds to wait before retrying
PROP_041.claim: When responder returns BUSY, initiator SHALL wait minimum delay before retry.
PROP_041.fsm_coverage: PARTIAL - FSM models R_Busy state but not initiator wait behavior
PROP_041.recommendation: Add initiator state with timer guard

PROP_042.name: LRU_Session_Eviction_Order
PROP_042.specification_quote: Use the SessionTimestamp to determine the least-recently used session
PROP_042.claim: Session eviction SHALL follow LRU order based on SessionTimestamp.
PROP_042.fsm_coverage: NOT MODELED - Requires multi-session state tracking

PROP_043.name: Concurrent_Exchange_Limit
PROP_043.specification_quote: A node SHOULD limit itself to a maximum of 5 concurrent exchanges over a unicast session
PROP_043.claim: Node SHOULD limit to 5 concurrent exchanges to prevent message counter window exhaustion.

GAP_001.title: Missing Initiator-Side PBKDF Parameter Validation

specification_evidence.page_169: Specification shows no validation between receiving PBKDFParamResponse and sending Pake1
specification_evidence.page_168: Responder has explicit validation: 'responder SHALL: 1. Verify passcodeID is set to 0'
specification_evidence.page_81: PBKDF iterations range defined but no validation mandate for initiator
specification_evidence.page_917: OpenCommissioningWindow iterations constraint (1000-100000) is CLUSTER-LEVEL not PASE-LEVEL

GAP_001.recommendation: Add initiator-side validation of PBKDF parameters with appropriate bounds checking

steps.description: Attacker controls rogue Matter device, opens commissioning window
Legitimate Commissioner initiates PASE, sends PBKDFParamRequest
Rogue device sends PBKDFParamResponse with iterations=1
Commissioner computes w0/w1 with weak PBKDF (no validation)
Attacker captures salt, pA, pB, cB from network

upgrade_summary.reason: FSM structure implicitly models correct key usage direction
FSM structure implicitly models correct key usage direction
